name: Cheng Chong (Kongli)
page_title: Cheng Chong (Kongli) 程冲 (孔禮) – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: A recluse, refused appointments from the Wei government in 235, 238, and 244.

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cheng Chong, styled Kongli, was from Fengqiu county, Chenliu commandery. He is not recorded in official history. He was said to have ambitious conduct, but when he was summoned in the years 235, 238, and 244 to serve in the central government, he declined them all. In 266, he died. He was recorded as being enfeoffed as District Marquis of Yuanxiang (元鄉亭侯), but it is unknown if this occurred when he was alive or if it was granted posthumously.

《六藝之一錄》曰：魏隱士程沖碑、節婦白氏碑，在封邱縣。按程隱君墳在縣西南四里，碑當在墓下。程冲字孔禮，陳留封邱人。有志行。明帝青龍三年徵不起。景初二年、正始五年徵，又不就。晉武帝泰始二年卒，封元鄉亭侯。記：白氏，沖妻也，晉咸熙中建，二碑同處。

full_text:
Cheng Chong (Kongli) 程冲 (孔禮)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cheng Chong (程冲), styledKongli (孔禮), was born in Fengqiu county, Chenliu commandery. A recluse, refused appointments from the Wei government in 235, 238, and 244. Cheng Chong lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Cheng Chong, styled Kongli, was from Fengqiu county, Chenliu commandery. He is not recorded in official history. He was said to have ambitious conduct, but when he was summoned in the years 235, 238, and 244 to serve in the central government, he declined them all. In 266, he died. He was recorded as being enfeoffed as District Marquis of Yuanxiang (元鄉亭侯), but it is unknown if this occurred when he was alive or if it was granted posthumously.
《六藝之一錄》曰：魏隱士程沖碑、節婦白氏碑，在封邱縣。按程隱君墳在縣西南四里，碑當在墓下。程冲字孔禮，陳留封邱人。有志行。明帝青龍三年徵不起。景初二年、正始五年徵，又不就。晉武帝泰始二年卒，封元鄉亭侯。記：白氏，沖妻也，晉咸熙中建，二碑同處。
